headline_cards.headline: Hazel AI triggers $150B wealth management sell-off
headline_cards.detail: Altruist launched Hazel, the first AI agent dedicated to tax planning for advisors. The market's reaction was immediate: LPL, Raymond James, Schwab, and Stifel all lost 7–9% in a single session — their worst days since 2020.
headline_cards.implication: Investors are pricing in AI-driven fee compression across wealth management. The question is no longer if AI disrupts advisory economics — it's when.

headline_cards.headline: LPL expands Anthropic partnership — Claude AI deploying to 30,000 advisors
headline_cards.detail: LPL Financial expanded its partnership with Anthropic in late February 2026, deploying Claude AI plugins to its network of 30,000+ financial advisors. The compliance-governed, data-controlled architecture is purpose-built for regulated wealth management.
headline_cards.implication: Independent advisors are no longer AI-disadvantaged vs. wirehouses. LPL's 30K advisor network with Claude AI is now a serious competitive force.

headline_cards.headline: Goldman Sachs and Anthropic partner on autonomous compliance agents
headline_cards.detail: Goldman Sachs announced a 6-month partnership with Anthropic to build autonomous AI agents for accounting automation, KYC, and client onboarding. The GS AI Assistant now spans 46,000 employees with >50% active usage.
headline_cards.implication: Goldman is moving from AI copilots to AI agents in compliance-sensitive workflows. If successful, this creates structural cost advantages in onboarding and KYC.

headline_cards.headline: DBS named World's Best AI Bank — 1,500 models, SGD 1B economic value
headline_cards.detail: DBS was awarded World's Best AI Bank by Global Finance for 2025. With 1,500+ AI models across 370+ use cases and projected SGD 1B in economic value, DBS has built the most comprehensive AI deployment in global wealth management.
headline_cards.implication: DBS is the reference architecture for AI in wealth management. Every APAC competitor — including JPM's IPB — is being measured against this benchmark.

headline_cards.headline: Arta AI expands institutional partnerships across Asia-Pacific
headline_cards.detail: Arta Finance expanded its AI agent suite to institutional partners including Bank of Singapore, Hong Leong Bank, and Ethivo Asset Management. The Advisor Sidekick product is enabling regional banks to deploy sophisticated AI without building in-house capability.
headline_cards.implication: AI-native platforms are building the infrastructure layer that regional banks will use to compete with global players. This accelerates AI adoption across the entire APAC wealth ecosystem.

talking_points: The February 2026 wealth management stock sell-off — triggered by a single AI product launch (Altruist's Hazel) — confirms that markets believe AI will structurally compress wealth management fees. This is no longer a technology question; it is a strategic and business model question.

Two distinct AI races are underway simultaneously: the advisor productivity race (Morgan Stanley 98%, Goldman 50%, UBS 30K users) and the business model disruption race (Altruist, Arta.ai, Robinhood Cortex). Winning one does not protect against the other.

DBS Bank's SGD 1 billion economic value from AI and 1,500 deployed models sets the benchmark for what AI-at-scale looks like. Every wealth manager in Asia-Pacific — including JPM's International Private Bank — is competing in DBS's home market.

stat_of_the_week.label: Wealth management market cap lost in one week
stat_of_the_week.context: February 10–12, 2026: LPL, Raymond James, Schwab, Stifel all fell 7–9% after Altruist's Hazel AI launch. Investors are pricing in AI-driven fee compression.
